title: Children's Rights Groups * Native American Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Children's Rights Groups * Native American Community 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NATIONAL INDIAN CHILD WELFARE ASSOCIATION CHILD ABUSE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral for children and families of Native American descent regarding issues of child abuse, custody, termination of parental rights, adoption, and foster care. Works to address the issues of child abuse and neglect through training, research, public policy, and grassroots community development. Also works to support compliance with the Indian Child Welfare Act of 1978. This act seeks to keep Native American children with Native American families. Does not provide legal advice or case management services. NICWA is not a direct service provider. 5100 S Macadam Avenue Suite 300 Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 222-4044 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL INDIAN CHILD WELFARE ASSOCIATION CHILD ABUSE SERVICES
